title: quercus virginiana subsp. fusiformis
intro: Quercus virginiana subsp. fusiformis, also known as the southern live oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree that is native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus virginiana subsp. fusiformis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
